19 min read
Just now

--

The internet feels like it has always existed. It hasn’t. Between you reading this sentence and a beeping metal ball in 1957 sits one of the strangest chains of accidents, panics, and giveaways in human history.
This post gives you three things most histories don’t:

• The full story, decade by decade, with the myths corrected

• The mental models behind each breakthrough — so you understand not just what happened, but why it worked

• A hands-on learning path at the end, so you can see every layer of this history running live on your own machine tonight
By the end, you won’t just know internet trivia. You’ll understand the machine you spend half your life inside.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The History of the Internet, Explained: From Sputnik to AI Slop (1957–2026)Before the Internet: The Fastest Network on Earth Was a Car
In the beginning, computers were lonely.
They could calculate, but they couldn’t talk. If you wanted to move data from one machine to another, you wrote it onto a magnetic tape, walked outside, got into your car, and physically drove it across town.
Engineers gave this a name: the sneakernet. And for roughly two decades, it was — without irony — the fastest data…
